title: senna silvestris subsp. bifaria
intro: Senna silvestris subsp. bifaria, also known as the twigs of Cassia senna, is a subspecies of the Senna plant native to Europe and North Africa. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides, which have been used for centuries to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. The health benefits of Senna silvestris subsp. bifaria include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome and hemorrhoids, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve liver function. Senna silvestris subsp. bifaria is often prepared as a dried herb, tea, or capsule for its medicinal purposes, although its use should be limited due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
